summary: Sort query-param keys before building the cacheKey hash in internal/client/client.go.
reason: The generator-emitted cacheKey iterated over the params map without sorting, so Go's randomized map iteration order made the same set of params produce a different sha256 digest each call. This made the 5-minute response cache miss almost every time on multi-param requests — agents repeatedly hammering an endpoint never benefited from the cache. Sort keys first so equivalent requests collide. Greptile P1 on PR #380.
